tools.name: env-create
tools.description: Launch a docker-compose stack from a local workspace directory as an ephemeral environment. Returns immediately with envId and status "creating" — the build happens in the background. Use env-status to poll until status is "running". The environment is isolated in its own Docker network scoped to your user.

localPath.description: Absolute path to the local workspace directory containing the docker-compose file. Example: /workspace/my-project

composeFile.description: Path to docker-compose file relative to localPath (default: docker-compose.yml)

envVars.description: Additional environment variables to inject into the compose stack (key-value pairs)

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for docker-compose up --build (default: 300)

tools.name: env-status
tools.description: Get detailed status of a Docker environment. Useful for polling after env-create until the environment is "running". Returns status, URLs, services, createdAt, error (if failed), and last build log lines. Possible statuses: creating, building, running, stopped, error.

buildLogLines.description: Number of recent build log lines to include in the response (default: 20, max: 100)

tools.name: env-exec
tools.description: Execute a command inside a running environment. The command runs in the context of the docker-compose project (e.g. `npx playwright test`, `npm run build`, `curl http://app:3000/health`). Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

service.description: Name of the service/container to run the command in (as defined in docker-compose)

command.description: Command to execute inside the container (e.g. "npx playwright test --reporter=json")

workdir.description: Working directory inside the container (optional, defaults to container default)

tools.name: env-logs
tools.description: Fetch recent logs from a service in a running environment. Returns the last N lines of container output.

service.description: Name of the service to get logs from (as defined in docker-compose). Omit to get logs from all services.

lines.description: Number of log lines to return (default: 50, max: 200)

timestamps.description: Include timestamps in log output (default: true)

tools.name: env-list
tools.description: List all environments for the current user/workspace. Shows envId, status (including intermediate states: creating, building, running, stopped, error), localPath, services, and URLs.

tools.name: env-restart
tools.description: Restart a Docker environment: stops all containers and brings them back up. Useful to reload config changes or recover from a stopped state. Returns immediately — use env-status to poll until status is "running".

rebuild.description: If true, rebuild images before restarting (equivalent to docker-compose up --build). Default: false.

tools.name: env-destroy
tools.description: Stop and remove a Docker environment: runs docker-compose down, removes containers, networks, and volumes. The local workspace directory (localPath) is NOT deleted. This action is irreversible.

removeVolumes.description: Also remove named Docker volumes (default: true)

tools.name: -health-check
tools.description: Internal health check. Verifies Docker socket connectivity.
